**Genomic Profiling Report: Cell Line X-123 (Preclinical Drug Sensitivity Analysis)**

Molecular profiling of cell line X-123 reveals a focal peak at 12p12.1 (GISTIC score +2) with an estimated *KRAS* copy number of 8-10. FISH analysis further confirms this, showing >12 *KRAS* signals per nucleus, and mRNA TPM for *KRAS* measures 487 (cohort median: 23). *TP53* and *CDKN2A* exhibit normal diploid copy numbers and expression levels within population medians. Experimental inhibition of *ETF1* in cell line X-123, using a targeted small molecule, resulted in an IC50 of 1.2 μM for cell viability. Similarly, shRNA-mediated depletion of *ETF1* led to 90% cell death by day 5. Notably, parallel experiments in control cell lines with typical *KRAS* copy numbers and expression levels showed no significant dependency on *ETF1* for survival.

Patient-derived organoids (PDOs) from a colorectal adenocarcinoma exhibited immunohistochemistry staining for APC protein at an IHC score of 0/3, with complete absence of nuclear signal. Genomic sequencing revealed a pathogenic variant in APC, c.640C>T (p.Arg214*), with a Variant Allele Frequency (VAF) of 89%, and concurrent Loss of Heterozygosity (LOH) at 5q22.2. TP53 and KRAS genes displayed wild-type sequences. When these PDOs were treated with small molecule inhibitor JQ-XYZ-47, specifically targeting VEGFA, cell viability plummeted to 8.2% at 72 hours, with 87% of cells staining positive for Annexin V. Crucially, control PDOs (wild-type for APC) treated identically showed no significant change in viability (95% ± 3%).

**Genomic Profiling Report - Drug Sensitivity Analysis**

**Sample ID:** P12345-T1 (Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma)

**Genomic Findings:**
Analysis of the tumor genome revealed a focal peak at 12p12.1 with an estimated copy number of 8-10 for *KRAS*. Transcriptomic analysis confirmed an *KRAS* mRNA z-score of +4.7 (relative to a cohort of n=50 healthy pancreatic tissues). *TP53* showed copy number neutral status, and *BRCA1* presented with TPM = 55 (cohort median: 50).

**Drug Sensitivity and Functional Genomics:**
In vitro studies on patient-derived xenografts (PDX) demonstrated a profound cellular dependency on *MPHOSPH10P1*. shRNA depletion kinetics targeting *MPHOSPH10P1* resulted in 90% cell death by day 5 in cell lines exhibiting the *KRAS* genomic profile described above. Conversely, cell lines with diploid *KRAS* status showed no significant cell death upon *MPHOSPH10P1* depletion. Furthermore, an investigational compound targeting *MPHOSPH10P1* yielded an IC50 of 1.2 μM exclusively in the *KRAS*-altered PDX models.

Patient-derived xenograft cells (PDX-P1) from a high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma were subjected to molecular and functional characterization. Genomic sequencing identified TP53 I255S, with a Variant Allele Frequency (VAF) of 89%, accompanied by Loss of Heterozygosity (LOH) at 17p13.1. Immunohistochemistry for TP53 demonstrated negative nuclear staining (IHC score 0/3). Genes ATM and PTEN displayed wild-type sequences and normal expression levels. When these PDX-P1 cells underwent CRISPR-Cas9 mediated disruption of CCT6B (targeting exon 3), cell viability dramatically reduced to 8.2% at 72h, with 87% of cells staining positive for Annexin V. In contrast, isogenic control PDX cells expressing wild-type TP53 maintained 95% viability upon identical CCT6B disruption.

**Specimen Type:** Patient-derived Glioblastoma Cell Line

**Molecular Profiling Findings:**
Molecular profiling identified ATM L2890V (c.8668C>G (p.Leu2890Val)). Targeted sequencing revealed a Variant Allele Frequency (VAF) of 89% and concomitant Loss of Heterozygosity (LOH) at 11q22.3. Immunohistochemistry demonstrated negative nuclear staining for ATM protein, and functional assays indicated ATM kinase activity <5% of reference levels. TP53 and KRAS sequencing were wild-type.

**Experimental Perturbation Analysis:**
In this cellular background, CRISPR-Cas9 mediated disruption of SSBP1 was performed. Control cells, maintaining intact SSBP1, exhibited robust viability (95% at 72h). However, SSBP1-disrupted cells displayed profound cellular demise, with viability declining to 8.2% at 72 hours, and 87% of cells staining positive for Annexin V, compared to 5% in controls. This effect was observed exclusively in cells with the described ATM status.
